summary: Narrative review of disruptive technologies in spine surgery including AR, VR, 3D printing, endoscopy, navigation, and robotics across pre-, intra-, and postoperative phases.
main_conclusion: These technologies enhance precision and outcomes but face challenges of high cost, limited accessibility, and need for rigorous validation.

summary: Translational rodent study investigating chemokine-mediated MSC mobilization and homing to intervertebral discs using intradiscal RANTES/CCL5 delivery with systemic AMD3100.
main_conclusion: Intradiscal RANTES combined with systemic AMD3100 recruited endogenous MSCs to the IVD and mitigated disc degeneration in a rat model.

summary: RCT comparing 3D-printed porous titanium vs PEEK cages in TLIF, stopped early due to significant interim results showing 100% vs 0% BSF-3 fusion at 6 months.
main_conclusion: 3D-printed porous titanium cages achieved significantly higher radiographic fusion rates than PEEK at 6 months (100% vs 0%, p<0.001).

summary: Cadaveric study evaluating 3D-printed navigation guide plates for radiation-free robotic thoracic pedicle screw placement across 168 screws in 7 cadavers.
main_conclusion: 98% of screws achieved acceptable Gertzbein-Robbins grades (85% Grade A), with mean deviation <0.5 mm, enabling accurate radiation-free placement.

summary: ML models developed to predict patient satisfaction 24 months after adult spinal deformity surgery using 213 patients and SHAP-based feature importance analysis.
main_conclusion: The predictive model achieved AUC 0.846 and accuracy 0.812, identifying 9 key modifiable indicators of postoperative satisfaction.

summary: Retrospective study developing ML models to predict early fusion after ACDF using 1039 segments from 840 patients, comparing 9 algorithms.
main_conclusion: Stochastic gradient boosting achieved the best performance (AUC 0.884 training, 0.830 testing) with FSU ROM, FPG, and BMD as key factors.

summary: Retrospective comparison of AR-guided vs freehand lumbosacral pedicle screw placement in 202 patients (1,109 screws) using CT-based Gertzbein-Robbins grading.
main_conclusion: AR navigation achieved significantly higher acceptable screw placement (99.3% vs 96.4%, p=0.013) with fewer clinically unacceptable breaches.

summary: Cadaveric study evaluating AR head-mounted display with 2D-3D registration for pedicle screw placement across 151 screws in cervical to sacroiliac regions.
main_conclusion: 97.4% of screws were accurately placed in the safe zone with angular errors <3 degrees and translational errors <3 mm.

summary: Narrative review of computer-assisted navigation in cervical spine surgery covering perioperative integration, system types, complications, and emerging technologies.
main_conclusion: CAN improves screw accuracy and reduces neurovascular complications in cervical surgery, though limited by learning curve and cost.

summary: Review of MIS-TLIF evolution covering expandable retractors, navigation technologies, expandable cages, awake techniques, and endoscopic approaches.
main_conclusion: Integration of navigation, robotics, and awake techniques continues to expand MIS-TLIF applicability with improved outcomes.

summary: Prospective comparison of robotic-assisted navigation vs AR for lumbosacral pedicle screw placement in 212 patients (1,211 screws) for degenerative conditions.
main_conclusion: Both achieved high accuracy (>98.7% acceptable), but RAN had significantly more Grade A placements than AR (95.2% vs 89.8%, p=0.001).

summary: Study of 247 patients examining Modic change progression and bony endplate defects after lumbar discectomy and their association with 1-year clinical outcomes.
main_conclusion: Type 1 Modic changes predicted residual low back pain at 1 year post-discectomy (beta 0.429, p<0.001) more than type 2 changes.

summary: Review of bioactive therapies for degenerative disc disease including MSC-based treatments, PRP, nucleus pulposus augmentation, hydrogel implants, and total disc replacement.
main_conclusion: Bioactive therapies show promise for pain relief and disc regeneration, but long-term safety and efficacy require further validation.

summary: Prospective RCT of 404 subjects evaluating intradiscal mesenchymal precursor cells with/without hyaluronic acid vs saline for chronic LBP through 36 months.
main_conclusion: MPC+HA showed significant pain reduction vs control, enhanced in patients with CLBP <68 months, though primary endpoints were not met.

summary: In vitro study assessing MSC-derived extracellular vesicles carrying miRNAs (miR-486-5p, miR-3648, miR-1827) for modulating autophagy in IVD degeneration.
main_conclusion: MSC-EVs with specific miRNAs downregulated SMAD2/ESR1/MAVS/MMP14, reduced apoptosis and autophagy, and enhanced cell proliferation in IDD.

summary: Retrospective review of 310 ASD patients assessing bone graft volume and composition as risk factors for pseudarthrosis, screw loosening, and rod breakage.
main_conclusion: Allograft bone <10.3 cc/level and DBM:bone ratio >1.5 were significant risk factors for mechanical failure after ASD correction.

summary: Retrospective cohort study of 36 pseudarthrosis revision cases vs 108 matched controls examining antidepressant use as a risk factor after lumbar fusion.
main_conclusion: SSRI/SNRI use was an independent predictor of revision for symptomatic lumbar pseudarthrosis (OR 3.95, 95% CI 1.66-9.45, p=0.002).

summary: Retrospective comparison of robotic-assisted vs freehand pedicle screw placement in 47 early-onset scoliosis patients examining accuracy and surgical outcomes.
main_conclusion: Robotic assistance achieved significantly higher screw accuracy (97.2% vs 79.4%, p<0.05) and reduced need for surgical plan modifications.

summary: Cadaveric study of AR head-mounted display-guided cervical pedicle screw placement (C3-C7) with 41 screws placed by 5 surgeons across 5 specimens.
main_conclusion: AR-guided cervical pedicle screws achieved 100% Gertzbein-Robbins Grade A/B with tip errors <3 mm and no neurovascular violations.

summary: Study of 54 patients characterizing lumbar cartilaginous endplate microbiota and metabolites via 16S rRNA sequencing and LC/MS in patients with and without Modic changes.
main_conclusion: Modic changes were associated with distinct microbiota profiles and dysregulated unsaturated fatty acid metabolism linked to specific genera.
